title: Trifolium rouyanum
taxonomy: Trifolium rouyanum is a species of clover, and its classification is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Magnoliophyta, the class Magnoliopsida, the order Fabales, the family Fabaceae, the genus Trifolium, and the species Trifolium rouyanum. The genus Trifolium is a group of legume plants commonly known as clovers. The family Fabaceae, to which Trifolium rouyanum belongs, is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. This classification helps to identify and understand the relationships between different plant species, including Trifolium rouyanum. Further research may be needed to confirm the accuracy of this classification and to learn more about the unique characteristics of Trifolium rouyanum.
uses: Trifolium rouyanum, also known as Rouyan's clover, is a species of clover found in the mountainous regions of Turkey.
